diary: Woke up in Sammamish to that gray, drizzly Tuesday light and immediately checked my messages like the rain might’ve changed everyone’s plans. I made espresso, pulled on my rain gear, and felt weirdly grateful that the day was at least consistent—steady rain from 7am to 2pm means I can plan instead of guess. The drive toward Bellevue was tense at first because the SR-520 bridge closure had everyone bunching up, but I routed around it and still got to Bellevue Downtown Park on time for the first dog walks and those indoor pet check-ins. Paseo Cafe at 9:45 felt like a tiny warm refuge. Later, Marymoor Park in Redmond was slick and quiet, and I kept everything extra clean. By the time I got home, I dried out, then did pottery/pastel while the rain finally tapered. I’m tired, but in a good, steady way.
thought_process: I chose to stay flexible but structured today because the world felt “locked in” by weather and traffic—rain from morning to early afternoon, plus SR-520 eastbound shut 9am-noon. That combination always makes me pad my timing, so I built extra cleanup supplies and a couple of leeway minutes into each walk. I also picked calmer routes even if they cost a few minutes, since stress makes me sloppy around pets. The bridge closure definitely raised my tension early, but once I was working in Bellevue and the shelters/indoor check-ins kicked in, my mood leveled out. At lunch I deliberately stopped and warmed up, because damp days drain me fast. By evening, doing pottery felt like reclaiming control—messy hands, quiet mind, rain softening outside.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
